text:
St Mary's Hospital 

Institute of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Ahmed  
St Mary's Hospital  
Institute of Neurology, St Mary's Hospital, Praed Street, London W2 1NY  

Dear Dr Ahmed,  

Sophie Bennett, DOB: 12-05-1991, Hospital No: H847392 NHS No. 5930182476  
Flat 3, 24 Arundel Road, London, W8 6JP 

Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1 g twice daily. Folic acid 5 mg once daily (pre-conception). Vitamin D3 1000 IU once daily (patient-initiated). 

Thank you for asking me to see Sophie Bennett for pre-conception counselling. She has been attending with ongoing seizures and is actively planning a pregnancy over the next 6–12 months. Over the last eight weeks she reports 2 to 4 clusters this month; each ~6 focal impaired-awareness seizures, typically occurring in the late afternoon or early evening. She describes brief behavioural arrest with right-hand automatisms and post-event confusion lasting 5–10 minutes, without clear generalisation. She notes that missed meals and poor sleep appear to be common precipitants. Her partner has witnessed several events and is supportive.

She has tolerated levetiracetam without mood lability or sedation and has not required emergency treatment. There is no recent history of status, head injury, or hospital attendance. She has not driven for the last year and is aware of DVLA guidance. Periods are regular; she has observed occasional clustering in the luteal phase but no consistent catamenial pattern.

We discussed pre-conception considerations including: the importance of daily folic acid 5 mg (already commenced), maintaining a regular sleep routine, and avoiding alcohol and sleep deprivation. We reviewed general safety advice around bathing, cooking, and heights. She has arranged a dental check and has started a pregnancy-safe multivitamin without vitamin A excess. Vaccinations are up to date by her report.

With respect to medication, she prefers to remain on levetiracetam given good tolerability and previous difficulties with sodium valproate in the family. She understands the balance of maintaining seizure control through pregnancy versus risks of medication exposure, and that abrupt changes are not advised. We will obtain a baseline serum levetiracetam level and plan to monitor levels each trimester, or sooner if clinical change occurs, as concentrations can fall in pregnancy. No additional antiepileptic medication changes are recommended at this stage in the absence of increased seizure burden. She will keep a seizure and trigger diary and bring it to her next appointment.

We discussed periconception planning, early referral to obstetric-led antenatal care with a named midwife, and a vitamin D check with your team if not done in the last year. She is aware of the need for prompt pregnancy notification to enable closer monitoring. We also discussed the importance of contraception reliability until she is ready to conceive; she intends to try from January and will continue folic acid until at least the end of the first trimester once pregnant.

Plan: 
- Continue levetiracetam 1 g twice daily. 
- Continue folic acid 5 mg once daily. 
- Baseline serum levetiracetam, FBC, U&E, LFTs, vitamin D via your surgery. 
- Seizure and trigger diary; emphasise sleep regularity and meal timing. 
- Early obstetric liaison when pregnancy confirmed; trimester levetiracetam levels. 
- Safety counselling reinforced; avoid swimming alone and unsupervised bathing. 

We will review her in three months or sooner if there is any increase in clusters or concerns. 

Yours sincerely

attributes.Label: 2 to 4 cluster per month, 6 per cluster

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "Over the last eight weeks she reports 2 to 4 clusters this month; each ~6 focal impaired-awareness seizures." This provides a monthly cluster frequency (2 to 4 per month) and the number of seizures per cluster (~6). The instruction is to prioritize explicit numeric expressions and use allowed formats. The '~6' can be normalized to '6' as an explicit numeric approximation, fitting the required format. Therefore, the highest frequency to report is the cluster frequency with per-cluster count: '2 to 4 cluster per month, 6 per cluster.' No other conflicting frequencies are provided.
seizure_frequency_number: 2 to 4 cluster per month, 6 per cluster
Over the last eight weeks she reports 2 to 4 clusters this month; each ~6 focal impaired-awareness seizures
reference: 2 to 4 cluster per month, 6 per cluster
